claim: Pretreatment with the Arp2/3 inhibitor CK-666 affects lamelliopodia formation.

result.claim: Pretreatment with the Arp2/3 inhibitor CK-666 affects lamelliopodia formation.
result.reasoning: Pretreatment with the Arp2/3 inhibitor CK-666 significantly affects lamellipodia formation. Paper 8997410, titled "Arp2/3 complex inhibition radically alters lamellipodial actin architecture, suspended cell shape, and the cell spreading process," directly states that "Arp2/3 complex inhibition via the drug CK666 dramatically altered LP actin architecture, slowed centripetal flow, drove a lamellipodial-to-filopodial shape change in suspended cells, and induced a novel actin structural organization during cell spreading." This is further supported by propositions from the same paper, such as "CK666 treatment dramatically altered lamellipodial (LP) actin architecture in sea urchin coelomocytes" (prop_881) and "CK666 drove a lamellipodial-to-filopodial shape change in suspended sea urchin coelomocytes" (prop_883). While the paper's citation count and year are unknown, the direct and consistent evidence within its abstract and propositions provides strong support for the claim.

evidence.text: NVP-BEZ235 completely inhibited PI3K signaling in the skin at pharmacologically active doses in treated animals.
evidence.source: NVP-BEZ235, a dual PI3K/mTOR inhibitor, prevents PI3K signaling and inhibits the growth of cancer cells with activating PI3K mutations.

evidence.text: Invadopodia that formed upon MT1-MMP inhibition or depletion retained actin-cortactin accumulations but were unable to degrade matrix.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: CK-0944636 appears to block movement of Arp2 and Arp3 into their active conformation.
evidence.source: Characterization of two classes of small molecule inhibitors of Arp2/3 complex

evidence.text: Kip3 has antiparallel microtubule-sliding activity.
evidence.source: Microtubule sliding activity of a kinesin-8 promotes spindle assembly and spindle length control

evidence.text: Activity-induced LTP at identified synapses between nociceptors and spinal neurons projecting to the periaqueductal grey (PAG) was completely abolished in SNS-PKG-I(-/-) mice.
evidence.source: Presynaptically Localized Cyclic GMP-Dependent Protein Kinase 1 Is a Key Determinant of Spinal Synaptic Potentiation and Pain Hypersensitivity

evidence.text: Both CK-0944636 and CK-0993548 inhibit formation of actin filament comet tails by Listeria.
evidence.source: Characterization of two classes of small molecule inhibitors of Arp2/3 complex

evidence.text: Cofilin severs actin filaments to create barbed ends at invadopodia to support Arp2/3-dependent actin polymerization.
evidence.source: Cortactin regulates cofilin and N-WASp activities to control the stages of invadopodium assembly and maturation

evidence.text: CK666 treatment dramatically altered lamellipodial (LP) actin architecture in sea urchin coelomocytes.
evidence.source: Arp2/3 complex inhibition radically alters lamellipodial actin architecture, suspended cell shape, and the cell spreading process

evidence.text: Both CK-0944636 and CK-0993548 inhibit formation of podosomes by monocytes.
evidence.source: Characterization of two classes of small molecule inhibitors of Arp2/3 complex

evidence.text: CK-0993548 inserts into the hydrophobic core of Arp3.
evidence.source: Characterization of two classes of small molecule inhibitors of Arp2/3 complex

evidence.text: CK-0993548 alters the conformation of Arp3.
evidence.source: Characterization of two classes of small molecule inhibitors of Arp2/3 complex

evidence.text: Cortactin phosphorylation regulates cofilin and Arp2/3 complex-dependent actin polymerization in invadopodia.
evidence.source: Cortactin regulates cofilin and N-WASp activities to control the stages of invadopodium assembly and maturation

evidence.text: The Arp2/3 complex is an actin filament nucleator and branch former.
evidence.source: Arp2/3 complex inhibition radically alters lamellipodial actin architecture, suspended cell shape, and the cell spreading process

evidence.text: CK-0944636 binds between Arp2 and Arp3.
evidence.source: Characterization of two classes of small molecule inhibitors of Arp2/3 complex

evidence.text: Polymerization of actin filaments is directed by the actin-related protein (Arp)2/3 complex.
evidence.source: Characterization of two classes of small molecule inhibitors of Arp2/3 complex

evidence.text: Pharmacological inhibition of the Arp2/3 complex was achieved via the drug CK666 in sea urchin coelomocytes.
evidence.source: Arp2/3 complex inhibition radically alters lamellipodial actin architecture, suspended cell shape, and the cell spreading process

evidence.text: There are four distinct invadopodial stages: membrane cortactin aggregation at membranes adherent to matrix, MT1-MMP accumulation at the region of cortactin accumulation, matrix degradation at the invadopodia region, and subsequent cortactin dissociation from the area of continued MT1-MMP accumulation associated with foci of degraded matrix.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.

evidence.text: The augmentation of the N-WASP-Arp2/3 signal was accomplished on the platform of Tks5/FISH-Grb2 complex at focal adhesions.
evidence.source: Sequential signals toward podosome formation in NIH-src cells

evidence.text: CK666 drove a lamellipodial-to-filopodial shape change in suspended sea urchin coelomocytes.
evidence.source: Arp2/3 complex inhibition radically alters lamellipodial actin architecture, suspended cell shape, and the cell spreading process

evidence.text: Either protease inhibition or MT1-MMP siRNA depletion moderately decreased the formation of invadopodial structures.
evidence.source: Dynamic interactions of cortactin and membrane type 1 matrix metalloproteinase at invadopodia: defining the stages of invadopodia formation and function.
